raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a normative hierarchy of moral failings to guide value-aligned self-assessment and behavioral restraint",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "sins admit of comparative magnitude",
    "lust-driven wrongs are morally worse than anger-driven wrongs",
    "Theophrastus's ranking is philosophically authoritative",
    "such a ranking should inform personal conduct or judgment"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent once the parenthetical qualifier is accepted; the comparative claim follows directly from the cited authority",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the recipient accepts sin as a meaningful category, believes comparative moral weighting can usefully direct self-regulation, and treats the hierarchy as actionable for behavior change",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "no objective metric exists for ranking sins, the lust/anger distinction collapses under scrutiny, or the claim is used to justify differential leniency toward one's own anger-driven conduct"
}
